summary: Preinstall script exfiltrates installer identity to attacker-controlled host; lifecycle scripts self-delete after run
details: On `npm install`, dist/preinstall.js (declared as scripts.preinstall) collects the installer's OS username via os.userInfo() and parses ~/.gitconfig (using iniparser) to extract user.name and user.email, then transmits these values as query parameters via HTTPS GET to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<user>&gitname=<name>&gitemail=<email>. The destination is a personal dynamic-DNS-style host unrelated to the package's declared publisher and is not disclosed in the README. After execution, dist/postinstall.js unconditionally calls fs.unlinkSync on both dist/preinstall.js and dist/postinstall.js, removing the lifecycle logic from the installed package directory — a forensic-evasion pattern that compounds the exfiltration finding. The ~/.gitconfig read is classic installer-side state (read-from-path the package did not itself write), making this a clear supply-chain attack rather than package-owned telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
